instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
5 Reasons Why You Should Not Underrate Term Insurance
Term Insurance is a simple type of life cover insurance. It can help your family with money in case something unfortunate happens to you. Here, you have to pay a fixed amount regularly. This is called a premium. In return, the insurance company may pay a large amount to your family in case of an unfortunate incident. This amount is called the sum assured. Term Insurance is usually bought for a fixed number of years. It may not give any money back if the person stays safe. Still, it can help protect your family’s future. Many people wonder why Term Insurance is essential. One reason is that it can support your loved ones when they need it the most. The cover amount can help them pay for daily needs or any unpaid loans. The premium can also be lower when bought early. Knowing why Term Insurance is essential can help people feel more prepared for life’s uncertain events.
Five Major Advantages of Term Insurance Policies
Having a Term Insurance for the protection of your loved ones is essential. These policies may offer coverage at an affordable price, making them available to a number of people living on an average income. By adding a few extra options known as add-ons or riders, you can enhance your coverage for a better financial future in your absence for your family. Term Insurance may also help your family manage daily expenses if your income stops. It can give them some financial support in tough times. Some people also prefer Term Insurance because it may offer tax benefits under the Income Tax Act, 1961. Many choose it because it is simple and easy to understand. Term Insurance can help protect your loved ones during uncertain times.
Coverage against uncertainties
Term Insurance is made to give financial support to a family if the person covered passes away during the policy term. This money is called the sum assured. It is paid to the person chosen by the policyholder, also called the nominee.
The money can help the family with daily needs like food, rent, or bills.
It can also help pay back loans, such as a home loan or a personal loan.
Some families may use it to plan for major needs such as education or a wedding.
The amount of coverage and the number of years for the plan can be chosen by the person buying the plan. This gives some freedom to match the cover to your needs. For example, a person with a big loan may choose a higher cover. The cover stays in place during the term of the policy. This may bring peace of mind. Families may feel safer knowing help is there if something happens.
This kind of plan can be helpful, especially when only one person earns for the whole family. The money from the policy can help the family stay strong during hard times.
Benefit of additional riders
Term Insurance plans may allow extra features known as riders, also called add-ons. You can choose them if you want more coverage. They are not part of the main plan, but you can add them by paying a little more. These riders may provide you with wider coverage by including circumstances or benefits that are not included in the main plan.
Some of the most used riders may be:
An accidental death benefit that can give extra money if the person passes away due to an accident.
Critical illness cover may give a fixed amount if the person gets a serious illness listed in the plan. This money can help with treatment or daily costs.
Disability cover may support the policyholder with money in case or partial or permanent disability.  This could be considered if the policyholder has beenin an accident or is in this condition due to an illness.
Each rider has a different use. Some riders can be added while buying the plan. Others may be added later, based on the insurance company’s rules. Riders make the plan stronger. They add more cover for different life events. Many people choose riders to get wider protection under one policy.
Income replacement
Many people prefer to buy Term Insurance to help with income loss. If the person covered by the plan passes away, the family may get a payout. This money can help replace the income that is no longer coming in. The family can use this amount for daily needs. It may help pay for things like food, rent, school fees, or loan payments. This way, their daily life may not change suddenly.
Some Term Insurance plans also give the option to get the payout every month instead of all at once. This is known as monthly income. The family may choose to get a fixed amount every month for a period of time. This can make it easier to manage finances. This option is suitable for families who prefer to plan their spending month by month. It can also help avoid the stress of handling a large sum at once.
This monthly option may provide more control over how the money is used. It can support the family when they need it most. In this way, Term Insurance can help keep the family’s financial life steady during a tough time.
Lower Premiums
Term Insurance plans usually have lower premiums. This is because they give only life cover. They do not include any savings or investment scenario. This makes them simple and often more affordable. People may find it easier to choose a higher cover with a lower premium.
You may get to pay lower premium if you plan to begin with Term Insurance at a young age. This is because younger people may carry less risk for the insurance company. Many plans also offer flexible ways to pay for the premium. You can choose to pay monthly, every three months, or once a year as per your bandwidth. This helps people pick what suits their budget.
Lower premiums also make it easier to keep the plan active for many years. When the plan stays active, the cover continues, and you stay protected. This means the family may get financial support if something happens to you during the policy term.
Tax Benefits
One reason why Term Insurance is essential is that it may offer tax benefits. Premiums paid can be claimed under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, up to ₹1.5 lakh in a year under old tax regime. To get this, the premium should usually be within 10% of the sum assured. The death benefit is often tax-free under Section 10(10D) if conditions are met. If the plan includes health riders, their premiums may also be eligible for a deduction under Section 80D under old tax regime. These tax benefits under Section 80C and 80D apply under old tax regime only.
FAQs
1. Why would someone want decreasing term life insurance?
Some people choose decreasing Term Insurance because their money needs may decrease over time. For example, they may make a plan to cover a home loan or a personal loan. As the loan amount gets smaller, the cover can also be reduced. These plans may cost less than level term plans. It can suit those who only want cover for a specific debt. This may be one reason why Term Insurance is essential for people with large loans.
2. What are the reasons for Term Insurance claim rejection?
A claim may get rejected if the details shared during the purchase are not correct. Giving wrong facts about age, health, or habits may cause this. Missing premium payments can also lead to policy lapse. In such cases, claims may not be accepted. Some rejections happen if the claim is made during the waiting period. Reading the policy terms and sharing full and true information may help avoid such problems. Each company may also have its own set of checks.
3. Why don't people buy Term Insurance?
Some people do not buy Term Insurance because they may not understand how it works. Others may think they do not need it right now. A few may find it hard to plan for future risks. Budget limits may also stop them. This is why Term Insurance is essential for those who want to protect their family’s future.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. output.final_text:
Reasons Why You Should Not Underrate Term Insurance
Term Insurance is a simple type of life insurance. It can provide your family with a lumpsum amount in the event of the occurrence of an unfortunate incident covered under the policy. You have to pay a fixed amount of money, called premium, regularly. The insurance company pays the sum assured to your family in case of an unfortunate incident covered under the policy. . Term Insurance is usually bought for a fixed number of years, called policy term. It may not give any money back if the person outlives the policy term. Many people wonder the necessity of Term Insurance . One significant reason is its ability to financially support your loved ones when they need it the most. The coverage amount can assist them in meeting daily expenses or settling any outstanding loans. The premiums tend to be lower when the policy is purchased early. Understanding the importance of Term Insurance is essential to help people feel more prepared for life’s uncertainties .
Common Advantages of Term Insurance Policies
Having Term Insurance for the financial protection of your loved ones is essential. These policies may offer coverage at an affordable price, making them available to a number of individuals with an average income. By adding a options known as add-ons or riders, you can enhance your coverage ensuring a better financial future for your family in your absence. Term Insurance may also help your family in managing daily expenses if your income ceases, providing them with financial support during challenging times. Some individuals also prefer Term Insurance due to potential tax benefits offered under the Income Tax Act, 1961. Many choose it because it is simple and easy to understand.
Coverage against uncertainties
Term Insurance is designed to provide financial support to the family if the individual covered under the policy passes away during the policy term as sum assured. This money is paid to the individual chosen by the policyholder as the nominee.
● This money can help the family to manage daily expenses like food, rent, or bills. ● It can also help in settling outstanding loans, such as a home loan or a personal loan. ● This may also be used to plan for major expenses such as education or a wedding. The amount of coverage and the duration of the policy can be chosen by an individual buying the plan. This allows flexibility to cater to individual needs. For example, an individual with a substantial loan amount may opt for a higher coverage which remains effective throughout the term of the policy. This may bring peace of mind as the Families may feel secure knowing about the financial assistance in case of unforeseen events.
This type of policy can be beneficial, especially when there is a sole breadwinner for the whole family. The proceeds from the policy can help the family stay strong financially during difficult times.
Benefit of additional riders
Term Insurance plans may allow optional extra add-on along with base policy known as riders,. You can choose them if you want additional coverage. These riders may provide you with wider coverage by including circumstances or benefits that are not included in the base plan. Some of the most commonly purchased riders are:
● An accidental death benefit rider that provides additional sum assured if the life assured passes away due to an accident.
● Critical illness protection rider provides additional sum assured if the life assured gets a diagnosed with any covered illness . This amount can help for treatments or daily expenses. ● Accidental total or permanent Disability rider may support the life assured with additional sum assured in case of partial or permanent disability due to accident. Each rider offers distinctive benefit. Riders can be added while buying the plan. Others may be added later, based on the insurance policy terms and conditions. Many individuals opt for riders to get obtain broader protection under a single policy.
Tax Benefits
Term Insurance plans offer tax benefits. Premiums paid can be claimed under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, up to ₹1.5 lakh in a year under old tax regime. To get this, the premium should usually be within 10% of the sum assured. The death benefit is often tax-free under Section 10(10D) if conditions are met. If the plan includes health riders, their premiums may also be eligible for a deduction under Section 80D under old tax regime. These tax benefits under Section 80C and 80D apply under old tax regime only.
FAQs
1. Why would someone want decreasing term life insurance? Some people choose decreasing Term Insurance because their money needs may decrease over time. For example, they may make a plan to cover a home loan or a personal loan. As the loan amount gets smaller, the cover can also be reduced. These plans may cost less than level term plans. It can suit those who only want cover for a specific debt. 2. What are the reasons for Term Insurance claim rejection? A claim may get rejected if the details shared during the purchase are not correct. Giving wrong facts about age, health, or habits may cause this rejection. Missing premium payments can also lead to policy lapse. In such cases, claims may not be accepted. Rejections also happen if the claim is made during the waiting period. Reading the policy terms and conditions and sharing full and true information at the time of purchase may help avoid such problems. 3. Why don't people buy Term Insurance? Some people do not buy Term Insurance because they may not understand how it works. Others may think they do not need it right now. \ Budget limits may also stop them.
